content.claim: Teacher shortages are concentrated in rural areas and linked to workforce aging: half of all Hungarian teachers in 2024/2025 are aged 50 or older, only 7.2% are under 30, and the proportion of unqualified teachers in disadvantaged (predominantly rural) schools is 2 to 3 times higher than in non-disadvantaged schools, constraining specialist-teacher capacity for early-selective tracks outside major cities.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Data does not disaggregate teacher qualifications or availability specifically for 6-/8-year track subjects (typically mathematics, language, science)
The causal link between teacher shortage and 6-/8-year track viability is inferred from general staffing constraints rather than direct track-level analysis
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
